title: Unit Final: Investing, Risk, and Wealth Building

choices.text: Investing is using money in a way that may increase its value over time
choices.feedback: Correct. Investing is intended to support future financial growth.

choices.text: Investing guarantees immediate profit
choices.feedback: Investing does not guarantee immediate profit.

choices.text: Investing removes all financial risk
choices.feedback: Investing still involves uncertainty and risk.

choices.text: Investing replaces the need for long-term planning
choices.feedback: Investing is stronger when it is part of long-term planning.

questions.prompt: If $900 grows to $1,020 over time, how much growth occurred?

choices.feedback: Subtract the starting amount from the later amount carefully.
Correct. $1,020 minus $900 equals $120 growth.
This is too high for the values in the comparison.
The growth amount is lower than $180.

questions.prompt: Risk and return should both be considered when making investment decisions.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong investment decisions compare both possible growth and possible risk.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Risk and return are both part of strong investment analysis.

questions.prompt: Which investment type is most closely connected to company ownership?

choices.text: Bonds
choices.feedback: Bonds are associated with lending rather than ownership.

choices.text: Mutual fund
choices.feedback: A mutual fund can contain many investments, but it is not the choice most directly tied to company ownership.

choices.text: Stocks
choices.feedback: Correct. Stocks are associated with ownership in a company.

choices.text: Transportation fee
choices.feedback: A transportation fee is not an investment type.

questions.prompt: Why can diversification be a stronger strategy than relying on one investment only?

choices.text: Because it guarantees every investment in the group will grow
choices.feedback: Diversification does not guarantee growth. Its value is in reducing dependence on one result.

choices.text: Because it guarantees that no investment will ever lose value
choices.feedback: Diversification can reduce some risk, but it does not guarantee no loss.

choices.text: Because it can reduce the risk of depending on one result
choices.feedback: Correct. Diversification spreads risk instead of concentrating it in one investment.

choices.text: Because it removes the need to compare options
choices.feedback: Investment options still need to be compared carefully.

questions.prompt: Scenario: One investor places all money in one investment with higher possible growth. Another spreads money across several investments for steadier growth. Which is the strongest conclusion?

choices.text: The single-investment choice is always stronger because it may grow faster
choices.feedback: Higher possible growth does not automatically make a one-investment strategy strongest.

choices.text: Neither option can be judged without ignoring risk
choices.feedback: Risk is one of the main factors that should be included in the judgment.

choices.text: The diversified choice is weaker because it compares more than one option
choices.feedback: Comparing more than one option can actually strengthen decision-making.

choices.text: The diversified choice can be stronger because it spreads risk across more than one option
choices.feedback: Correct. Diversification can reduce the risk of depending on one result.

questions.prompt: Earlier investing can provide more time for growth to build.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Earlier investing can strengthen growth because time allows more value to build.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Time can make earlier investing stronger for long-term growth.

questions.prompt: Scenario: Option A grows from $1,000 to $1,120. Option B grows from $1,000 to $1,210 over a longer time. Which conclusion is strongest?

choices.text: Option A is stronger because the starting amounts are the same
choices.feedback: The stronger comparison depends on the actual growth outcome, not only the starting amount.

choices.text: Neither option can be compared because the time periods are different
choices.feedback: They can still be compared by using the evidence given about growth.

choices.text: Option B is stronger because it produced $210 growth, which is more than Option A's $120 growth
choices.feedback: Correct. Option B shows the stronger growth outcome in this comparison.

choices.text: Option A is stronger because shorter time always matters more than total growth
choices.feedback: Time matters, but the stronger conclusion here uses the growth evidence provided rather than assuming shorter time always wins.

questions.prompt: Scenario: A person wants a long-term retirement-focused investment option. Which choice is most closely aligned to that goal?

choices.text: Checking account
choices.feedback: A checking account is not the choice most closely aligned with a long-term retirement-focused investment goal.

choices.text: Sales tax category
choices.feedback: A sales tax category is not an investment option.

choices.text: One-time transaction fee
choices.feedback: A transaction fee is not an investment type.

choices.text: Retirement account
choices.feedback: Correct. A retirement account is closely connected to long-term future planning.

questions.prompt: Scenario: An investor wants long-term growth but also wants to reduce reliance on one single outcome. What is the strongest overall strategy?

choices.text: Ignore all risk and focus only on the highest possible return
choices.feedback: Strong investment decisions should compare risk as well as possible return.

choices.text: Use diversification and long-term planning together
choices.feedback: Correct. A diversified long-term strategy can support growth while reducing dependence on one single result.

choices.text: Choose one investment only and avoid comparing alternatives
choices.feedback: Avoiding comparison can weaken investment decision-making.

choices.text: Judge the decision without considering time
choices.feedback: Time is an important part of long-term investment growth.
